marketState: bearish — seasonal headwinds, storage surplus, and rapidly fading weather demand outweigh extreme short-side COT positioning
summary: The Aug 2026 front contract at $3.279 sits modestly above the fair-value midpoint ($2.960) but remains inside the upper band edge ($3.460), so price is mildly rich rather than at an extreme. Near-term (1–7d) power-burn demand is historically extreme — above the 5-year max at rangePos 1.8 and +52.5% vs 5y — but is already collapsing sharply (−19.9 Bcf/d in 24 hours), and the 8–16-day outlook drops back to a benign +6% vs the 5-year norm (rangePos 0.71), stripping out the weather premium that has supported current price. Seasonality is a clear structural headwind over the next four weeks: only a 19% probability of a higher price, a typical 4-week move of −1.8%, and historically low reliability. Storage at +151 Bcf above the 5-year average adds a fundamental bearish anchor; the sole meaningful bull counterpoint is managed-money positioning at the 18th 5-year percentile — near historically extreme short — which raises short-squeeze risk and trims conviction on the short side.
